name: Ria de Ares - Mugardos Return
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Ares are characterized by a diverse coastal landscape, featuring estuaries, beaches, and rolling hills. The region offers varied terrain, from flatter sections along the rias to more challenging climbs inland. Elevations reach over 1300 meters, providing a dynamic environment for road cyclists. This combination includes both scenic coastal stretches and undulating rural roads. Best road cycling routes around Ares The most popular road cycling route is Ria de Ares - Mugardos Return, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Ares estuary and connects coastal towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Playa Grande de Miño – Ponte Vella loop from Pontedeume, a difficult 40.1 miles (64.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, traversing varied landscapes between coastal areas and inland villages. Local road cyclists also love the Puntal – O Roxal loop from Mugardos, a 27.9 miles (45.0 km) trail leading through coastal and hilly terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Ares is defined by coastal paths, estuarial views, and challenging hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides along the water to difficult routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Ares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Ares?
A: There are over 130 road cycling routes around Ares, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Ares?
A: Road cycling routes in Ares feature a diverse coastal landscape with estuaries, beaches, and rolling hills. You'll find varied terrain, from flatter sections along the rias to more challenging climbs inland, with some routes reaching over 1300 meters in elevation.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ares?
A: Yes, Ares offers 18 easy road cycling routes. A good option for those looking for a moderate but accessible ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963383221/ria-de-ares-mugardos-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ria de Ares - Mugardos Return&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.4 miles (16.8 km) long and offers scenic views of the estuary.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes in Ares?
A: For advanced cyclists, Ares has 45 difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39550487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal – Xuvia Mill loop from Cabanas-O Areal&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 64.3 miles (103.5 km) route with over 1390 meters of ascent, providing a demanding experience.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Ares?
A: Yes, many routes in Ares are designed as loops. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15015467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Grande de Miño – Ponte Vella loop from Pontedeume&lt;/a&gt; is a 40.1 miles (64.5 km) circular path, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39552480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal – O Roxal loop from Mugardos&lt;/a&gt; covers 27.9 miles (44.9 km).

Q: What scenic viewpoints or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning coastal views and pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6599577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magnolia of Xuvia&lt;/a&gt; or historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3546198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Martiño de Xuvia&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3850169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The White Pebble&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Ares?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 41 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse coastal paths, estuarial views, and the variety of routes catering to different ability levels, from moderate rides along the water to difficult routes with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by beaches or coastal areas?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its coastal landscape. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15015467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Grande de Miño – Ponte Vella loop from Pontedeume&lt;/a&gt; incorporate coastal sections, and you can even find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Grande de Miño&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: Can I find routes that connect different towns or villages?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to connect coastal towns and inland villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963383221/ria-de-ares-mugardos-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ria de Ares - Mugardos Return&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, links Ares with Mugardos, offering a great way to explore the local communities by bike.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Ares?
A: One of the longest routes is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39550487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal – Xuvia Mill loop from Cabanas-O Areal&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 64.3 miles (103.5 km) and typically takes over 5 hours to complete, offering an extensive ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the region around Ares is rich in history. Cyclists can pass by sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7469393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of St James in Pontedeume&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/750586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Executed in the Civil War&lt;/a&gt;, offering opportunities to combine cycling with cultural exploration.
tips: Artisanal fishing in the Ria de Ferrol. Stop for a coffee or try the seafood for lunch
A tiny fishing village at the end of the Ares estuary

waypoints.name: Mugardos Waterfront and Pulpo a la Mugardesa
